joined_reference: directing with a sure and measured hand, [haneke] steers clear of the sensational and offers instead an unflinching and objective look at a decidedly perverse pathology. for all the complications, it's all surprisingly predictable. becomes the last thing you would expect from a film with this title or indeed from any plympton film : boring. charly comes off as emotionally manipulative and sadly imitative of innumerable past love story derisions. a chronicle not only of one man's quest to be president, but of how that man single-handedly turned a plane full of hard-bitten, cynical journalists into what was essentially, by campaign's end, an extended publicity department. impossible as it may sound, this film's heart is even more embracing than monty, if only because it accepts nasty behavior and severe flaws as part of the human condition. blue crush is as predictable as the tides.... the movie feels stitched together from stock situations and characters from other movies. why come up with something even quasi-original, when you can pillage from shirley jackson, richard matheson... and puke up something like rose red?
